title: Output dependencies in Makefile format?
body:
Is there a way to have the `-M` option output dependencies in a format suitable for `make` to process, similar to how `yosys -E` or `gcc -MF` do it?  Without this there is a post-processing step needed to transform the list of files into something that can be included.

My current work around is to rewrite the file after the `iverilog` invocation by adding the target file name and continuation characters on each line:

```
%.vvp: %.v
	iverilog -DSIM=1 -M.$@.d -o $@ $<
	@perl -ni -e 'print "$@: \\\n" if $$. == 1; chomp; print "$$_ \\\n"' .$@.d
	@chmod -x $@

-include .*.d
```
